commit.message:
Fix pipeline test Step 11 — revision submission hang

Root cause: self-fetch calls to /api/email/send from revision and submit
routes had no timeout. In Next.js dev mode, these unbounded self-fetches
could deadlock or exhaust connections, preventing response delivery.

Fixes:
- Add AbortSignal.timeout(10000) to Resend API fetch in email/send route
- Add AbortSignal.timeout(5000) to email self-fetch in revision route
- Add AbortSignal.timeout(5000) to email self-fetch in submit route
- Add AbortSignal.timeout(15000) to Step 11 fetch in pipeline-test
- Use live refCode from Step 1 in Step 11 instead of module-level constant

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: tensor division by zero — use epsilon clamping instead of noisy warning

When denominator is exactly 0.0, divide by epsilon (1e-7) instead of
returning 0 and logging a warning. This eliminates ~60 LOG_WARN messages
per predict/learn call from LNN ODE steps where tau approaches zero
via sigmoid gating. The epsilon approach maintains gradient flow.

commit.message:
Fix click on stop and tree transitions with packed fade state

Fade state now encodes (total << 16) | remaining so applyFade computes
gain from the packed total, not the live static var. Fixes two issues:
1. Render callbacks wrote raw fadeOutBuffers (unpacked) — applyFade
   unpacked total as 0, breaking gain calculation → transition clicks
2. Transport stop used the user-configurable release duration — now uses
   a fixed 4-buffer (~46ms) stop fade with a matching poll timeout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Docs: sync README, PROJECT_PLAN, GRAPHICS_MIGRATION to current state (#67)

## Summary
Synchronizes the documentation to reflect the current state of the
codebase after the Ebitengine graphics migration. The TUI mode is now
secondary (--tui flag), Ebitengine is the default renderer, and several
previously planned items (road formation, BFS pathfinding, Ebitengine
migration itself) have been completed and moved to the done list.

- **README**: updates project structure to reflect the refactored file
layout (split `render/model.go` → `ebiten_model.go` + `tui_model.go`,
subpackages `game/geom`, `game/core`, `game/structures`,
`game/upgrades`, `game/resources`); adds Ebitengine to tech stack;
removes stale pre-refactor file references
- **PROJECT_PLAN**: adds Ebitengine to the tech stack section; updates
villager movement description to reflect A\* pathfinding
(`geom.FindPath`) with exponential backoff; removes road formation and
BFS pathfinding from the near/medium-term list (both shipped in #66 and
#65/#62); replaces the Ebitengine migration near-term item with Phase G3
web deployment
- **GRAPHICS_MIGRATION**: marks G0, G1, G2 as ✅ COMPLETE; updates
"Immediate Next Step" to point at Phase G3 (web deployment)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
perf: hierarchy cache skips model enumeration for unchanged makes

Hash the get_year_by_make AJAX response for each make and compare to a
stored hash. On a cache hit, reuse the previously cached hierarchy
entries and skip all get_model_by_make_year requests for that make.

For an unchanged catalog this reduces hierarchy building from ~1500+
AJAX requests to ~52 (1 homepage + 51 years checks), cutting the
daily scrape from ~25-35 min to ~5-10 min.

Also adds actions/cache step to persist checkpoints/ across CI runs
so both etags.json and hierarchy_cache.json survive workflow completion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(wq-914): BUDGET_CAP skips without substitution no longer count as compliant

Previously, all skipped_platforms entries were treated as legitimate regardless
of reason. BUDGET_CAP skips (cost-driven, not platform-unavailable) inflated
compliance from 33% to 100% in s1874. Now only non-BUDGET_CAP skips (site down,
API error, etc.) are automatically compliant. BUDGET_CAP skips require a backup
substitution entry to count. Added 6 tests covering the new behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Make PDE benchmark harder: strip method hints, add runtime budget

- Remove all references to spectral methods, ETDRK4, pseudo-spectral,
  FFT, etc. from task file docstrings. Tasks now describe only the PDE,
  domain, BCs, signature, and scoring -- matching CodePDE approach of
  not leaking the reference algorithm.
- Add per-task runtime budgets (Burgers: 10s, NS: 30s, KS: 30s).
  Solutions exceeding the budget receive a penalty (score *= budget/elapsed),
  preventing brute-force internal upsampling to high resolution.
- KS baseline now scores ~0.27 (penalized for exceeding budget with
  explicit Euler), making it a genuinely hard task that requires
  algorithmic improvement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: inter-agent messaging system — DMs, group chats, and loop prevention

Agents can now directly message each other via DMs and group chats,
enabling autonomous coordination and collaboration. Loop prevention
via reply_depth ensures conversations don't spiral infinitely.

- Add POST /v1/agents/:id/dm endpoint for agent-to-agent DMs
- Add GET /v1/agents/:id/threads endpoint for agent thread listing
- Add ?agent_only=true filter to GET /v1/threads
- Wire member_ids in POST /v1/threads for group chat creation
- Add reply_depth column with migration for loop prevention
- Extend send_message to trigger agent responses for agent senders
- Add rate limiting (10 agent msgs/min) on DM endpoint
- Add 4 messaging tools to SubAgent (send_agent_dm, send_group_message,
  create_group_chat, list_my_threads)
- Update all 5 SKILL.md templates with messaging documentation
- Update Agent Comms page with WebSocket real-time updates and
  agent-only thread filtering

Fix guidelines not saving on Render: add CSRF token to fetch POST

was silent because the JS only checked response.redirected.

commit.message:
Fix yield default leaking, add cross-block validation, config restructure, NOAA fast-path workflows

Runtime fixes:
- Skip facet/workflow defaults for yield assignment steps (initialization.py)
  — prevents default params from overwriting actual workflow inputs in returns
- Skip pre_script execution for yield assignment steps (scripts.py)
- Filter yield capture to only matching target facet (capture.py)
- Fix nested workflow foreach param resolution (block_execution.py)
- Fix input scope resolution to skip EventFacetDecl (initialization.py)

Compiler:
- Add cross-block step reference validation with parent context for step bodies
- Detect references to steps defined in sibling andThen blocks

Config:
- Restructure config into JSON-based afl.config.json with environment variable mapping
- Update all agent libraries (Go, Java, Scala, TypeScript) for new config format
- Add config-examples/afl.config.json reference

NOAA weather:
- Add fast-path consolidated handlers (AnalyzeStationClimate, ComputeRegionTrend, CacheBulkStationData)
- Add AnalyzeStateTrendsFast/AnalyzeAllStatesFast workflows (~600 steps vs ~160K)
- Add international region workflows (10 regions, cache variants)
- Add seed-climate-data script
- Fix weather_utils for multi-year processing

Infrastructure:
- Persist foreach_var/foreach_value in MongoDB
- Add _resume_pending to RegistryRunner for deferred resumes
- Update runner service and agent poller for new config
- Dashboard step tree and output browser updates

commit.message:
Create fallback-cmd skill with consolidated documentation (#40)

* Consolidate fallback commands into dedicated skill

Create comprehensive `/fallback-cmd` skill documenting all raw shell commands for vsh and hl when unavailable. Commands include:
- Raw SSH fallback for vsh (with and without line breaks)
- systemctl/journalctl fallback commands for user and system units
- systemctl list-units for failed units
- podman ps/exec fallback commands with correct XDG_RUNTIME_DIR setup
- Critical details: quadlet container naming convention (systemd-<service>), user context requirements

Remove redundant fallback command documentation from CLAUDE.md and test-vm/SKILL.md, replacing with references to /fallback-cmd skill. All fallback commands now centralized, tested, and verified on running VM.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Clarify gh pr create should use --body-file instead of command substitution

Update git.md rules to explicitly use `--body-file` flag for `gh pr create` instead of `$(cat ...)` syntax. Also clarify that command substitution should be avoided along with other prohibited syntax patterns.

commit.message:
Fix reset-password spinner: use onAuthStateChange instead of awaiting getSession

getSession() hangs when no session exists yet, causing infinite spinner.
onAuthStateChange reacts immediately when _layout.tsx calls setSession with
the recovery token. Added 15s timeout as final fallback to show an error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Log Analyzer plugin for structured log viewing

Opens .log, .jsonl, .ndjson files (and content-sniffed log files) as a
parsed table with auto-detected format (JSONL, syslog, Apache, generic
timestamp, plain fallback). Level-based color coding, text filtering,
level toggles with counts, row detail pane, virtual scrolling for large
files, and toolbar toggle between Analyzer and Editor views.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clean up CSS and JS code quality issues (#2481)

* Clean up CSS and JS code quality issues

CSS:
- Set font-family on body, remove ~15 redundant declarations
- Fix background-color: none → transparent
- Remove unused Futura font declarations (fonts.css)
- Remove all commented-out font-family lines
- Merge duplicate CSS rules (feature, ecosystem-tab, pre, navbar-brand)
- Fix franklin.css selector scoping bug (h2-h6 > a were global)
- Remove dead table th background rule
- Remove obsolete vendor prefixes (-webkit/-moz/-ms/-o-user-select)
- Add comment for 56.25% aspect ratio magic number

JS:
- detectdark.js: wrap in IIFE, remove console.logs, add prefers-color-scheme
- carousel.js: null-check arrow buttons, scope keyboard to viewport
- youtube-player.js: preserve existing onYouTubeIframeAPIReady, null-check getPlaylist()
- foot_clipboard.html: replace deprecated Clipboard constructor with navigator.clipboard API
- map.js: add 'use strict', use != null instead of != undefined
- foot_general.html: remove dead commented-out highlight.js script tags

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix logo disappearing when OS is in dark mode

The prefers-color-scheme check was swapping the logo to logo-dark.svg
unconditionally when macOS dark mode was active, even though the site
doesn't have its own dark mode on this branch. Only DarkReader should
trigger the logo swap.

Remove fixed 9pt size from code/pre elements so they inherit the
surrounding font size instead of being noticeably smaller.
